quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: Hungarian paprika isn't just one spice — there are actually eight official grades ranging from mild and sweet to hot and pungent! The town of Szeged and Kalocsa are so famous for paprika production that they have special protected status, like champagne from France.

short_read.title: The Secret Life of Olympic Marmots
short_read.teaser: These charming whistling mammals are found nowhere else on Earth except the Olympic Mountains. Learn about their playful families and how they survive the long mountain winters.

medium_read.title: Why Gardens Are Good for Your Brain
medium_read.teaser: Scientists are discovering that gardening does more than grow beautiful flowers — it actually helps keep our minds sharp and our spirits lifted. This gentle article explores the connection between soil, plants, and happiness.

long_read.title: The Lost Gardens of Europe: Stories from Historic Estates
long_read.teaser: Take a peaceful journey through time visiting some of Europe's most beautiful historic gardens, from English rose gardens to Hungarian palace grounds. Each garden tells a story of the people who loved and tended it through the centuries.

port_angeles.title: Harbor Seal Pupping Season at Ediz Hook
port_angeles.description: Late June and early July is when harbor seal mothers give birth to their pups along the protected waters of Ediz Hook. You might spot these adorable baby seals learning to swim while their mothers keep a watchful eye. The best viewing is from a respectful distance with binoculars.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart can beat up to ___ times per minute during flight.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Amazing! You're exactly right — a hummingbird's tiny heart is an incredible powerhouse!
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! These tiny hearts beat about 1,200 times per minute — that's 20 times per second!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: Those tiny hearts are faster than you'd think — about 1,200 beats per minute! That helps power their incredible hovering flight.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: To fuel this rapid heartbeat, hummingbirds must eat about half their body weight in nectar every day and visit hundreds of flowers. Their metabolism is so fast that they enter a hibernation-like state every night to conserve energy.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Wonderful! Pillangó is such a beautiful word for these delicate creatures!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The Hungarian word for butterfly is 'pillangó' — such a lovely, musical word! (Virág means flower, madár means bird, and csillag means star.)
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian is unique among European languages as it belongs to the Finno-Ugric language family, making it more closely related to Finnish and Estonian than to neighboring languages. Many Hungarian words have a musical, flowing quality.

puzzle_3.question: What special feature do deer have that helps them see predators approaching from almost any direction?
options: They can turn their heads 360 degrees
Their eyes are positioned on the sides of their head
They have mirrors in their eyes
They can see through their antlers
puzzle_3.correct_response: Perfect! Deer's eyes give them nearly 310-degree vision, letting them spot danger while grazing. The Olympic Peninsula's black-tailed deer use this gift every day!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: It's those side-positioned eyes! This gives deer about 310 degrees of vision, with just a small blind spot directly behind them. It's perfect for spotting predators while peacefully grazing.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: Black-tailed deer are common throughout the Olympic Peninsula and often visit gardens at dawn and dusk. They're smaller cousins of mule deer and are native to the Pacific Coast from California to Alaska.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
